title: Present Simple

videoQuiz1.text: Is this action happening right now or does it happen every day?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
We use Present Simple to talk about everyday actions, habits, and routines.
Examples: I brush my teeth everyday. He washes the dishes everyday.

videoQuiz1.text: Do Peppa and Jack sleep with their toys every day?
videoQuiz1.explanation: Examples: When Peppa goes to bed, she always has her teddy.

videoQuiz1.text: Does the video show a routine or habits?
videoQuiz1.explanation: When Peppa goES to bed, she always haS her teddy (always, repetitively), which means she does this everyday.

videoQuiz1.text: Reorder the words to form a correct sentence:
videoQuiz1.correct: Peppa goes to bed with her toy everyday.
videoQuiz1.explanation: The correct word order: Peppa goes to bed with her toy everyday. In Present Simple affirmative sentences: Subject + verb (+-s for he/she/it) + the rest.

videoQuiz1.text: Reorder the words to form a correct sentence:
videoQuiz1.correct: Jack goes to bed with his dinosaur everyday.
videoQuiz1.explanation: The correct word order: Jack goes to bed with his dinosaur everyday. Subject + verb + object/phrase.

videoQuiz1.text: Do the verbs change their form when we talk about he / she / it?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
In Present Simple we add "-s/-es" to verbs for 3rd person singular in affirmative sentences.
Example: When Peppa goes to bed, she always haS her teddy.

videoQuiz1.text: Do the verbs change their form if the subjects are not “he/she/it”?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
In Present Simple verbs do not change their form when we talk about two or more people. In Present Simple verbs also do not change their form for “I” and “You”.
Example: I like stories. Peppa and Jack like stories.

videoQuiz1b.text: Peppa and Jack _________ their teeth in the bathroom.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: Peppa and Jack = they (plural) → use the base form: brush, no -s ending.

videoQuiz1b.text: Peppa _________ to bed in the evening.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: Peppa = she (3rd person singular) → add -s: go → goes.
Peppa and Jack = they (plural) → base form: like, no -s.

videoQuiz1b.text: When Peppa goes to bed, she always _________ her teddy with her.
Jack _________ Mr. Dinosaur tucked up with him.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: Jack = he (3rd person singular) → has (irregular: have → has).

videoQuiz1b.text: Jack _________ in the top bunk bed.
Peppa _________ in the bottom bunk bed.
Mummy Pig and Daddy Pig _________ the children get ready for bed.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: Mummy Pig and Daddy Pig = they (plural) → base form: help.

grammarBank1.title: Present Simple +
grammarBank1.intro: We use the present simple for things that are generally true or are habits.
grammarBank1.structure: The word order for present simple is SV = Subject + verb
examples: We add -s to the verbs when the subjects are 3rd person singular.
Jack goes to bed with his dinosaur.

Ilike an apple
Youlike an apple
Helikes an apple
Shelikes an apple
Itlikes an apple
Welike an apple
Theylike an apple

videoQuiz2.text: Pay attention to the example: “I do not remember him”, “He doesn’t have a problem with me”.
What auxiliary verbs do we add to make the sentence negative?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
We use auxiliary verbs “don’t” and “doesn’t” to make negative statements in the Present Simple.
Idon’t like apples
Youdon’t like
Hedoesn’t like
Shedoesn’t like
Itdoesn’t like
Wedon’t like
Theydon’t like

videoQuiz2.text: Where do we put auxiliary verbs “don’t” and “doesn’t” in the sentence?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Examples: I don’t eat breakfast. She doesn’t go to school.

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in correct order:
videoQuiz2.explanation: Word order: Subject + don’t/doesn’t + verb + object. They don’t remember Will.

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in correct order:
videoQuiz2.explanation: Rachel = she (3rd person singular) → doesn’t + verb (no -s on the main verb). Rachel doesn’t like him.

videoQuiz2.text: Look at the sentence “He doesn’t have a problem with me”. What is the form of the main verb in a negative sentence in the Present Simple?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
In negative sentences we use the base form of the verbs for all subjects.
George doesn’t drive a car. Peppa doesn’t ride a bicycle.

videoQuiz2.text:
Look at the dialogue:
Ross: Does he have a problem with you?
Rachel: I don’t know. Do you have a problem with me?
Will: I don’t know. Do I? Do I?
What auxiliary verbs start the question?
Where is the subject in the question?
Put the words in correct order:
videoQuiz2.correct: Do you have a problem with me?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Word order for questions: Do/Does + subject + verb + …? Do you have a problem with me?

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in correct order:
videoQuiz2.explanation: Will = he (3rd singular) → Does + subject + verb (no -s). Does Will remember Rachel?

videoQuiz2.text: Look at the question “Does he have a problem with you?”. The main verb is …
videoQuiz2.explanation:
The main verbs don’t change their form in negative and interrogative sentences.
Examples: Does he walk to school? He doesn’t walk to school.

videoQuiz2.text: Which auxiliary verb goes with “I / You / We / They”?
Which auxiliary verb goes with “He / She / It”?

Look at the dialogue between Rachel, Will and Phoebe:
Rachel: Excuse me, do you have a problem with me?
Will: I don’t know. Do I? Do I?
Phoebe: I think you do.
Does Phoebe repeat the whole sentence to give a short answer?
What auxiliary word does Phoebe use to give a short answer?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
To give short answers we use only subject and auxiliary verbs.
Example: Do you have a problem with me? Yes, I do. / No, I don’t.
Does Rachel remember Will? Yes, she does. / No, she doesn’t.

blocks.intro:
NEGATIVE SENTENCES
To make negatives in Present Simple we use don’t / doesn’t + verb (infinitive).
The word order for negatives is SAI = Subject + auxiliary verb/not + infinitive

Contractions: do not = don’t    does not = doesn’t

QUESTIONS
To make questions in Present Simple we start the sentence with Do / Does.
The word order for interrogatives is ASI = Auxiliary verb + Subject + Infinitive?

Do and does can be: the auxiliary verb to make negatives and questions: Do you like him? Does he remember you? — or a normal verb: He does his homework every day.
examples: Does he have a problem with Rachel?

videoQuiz2b.explanation: Will = he (3rd singular) → does not like (no -s on the main verb).
Rachel = she → does not understand (no -s on the main verb).

videoQuiz2b.text: Phoebe _________ that everything is fine between them.

videoQuiz2b.explanation: Monica = she (3rd singular) → does not keep (no -s on the main verb).
Will and Rachel = they (plural) → do not get.
Ross = he (3rd singular) → does not explain (no -s on the main verb).
Will = he (3rd singular) → does not act (no -s on the main verb).

videoQuiz2b.text: _________ Rachel remember Will from high school?
_________ Joey know the man at the table?
_________ Will have a problem with Rachel?
_________ Will and Rachel like each other?

videoQuiz3.text:
Look at the timetable examples from the video:
Guard’s doors open at 8:15.  Train leaves at 10.
Are these actions happening right now, or are they scheduled for the future?
videoQuiz3.explanation: We use Present Simple for fixed, scheduled future events — like timetables and official programmes.

videoQuiz3.text: Are these plans flexible (like “I might see a movie”), or fixed official schedules?
videoQuiz3.explanation: We use the Present Simple to talk about guaranteed, scheduled facts in the future.

videoQuiz3.text:
Look at the affirmative sentence:
Train leaves at 10.
What ending does the verb get because “train” is 3rd person singular (it)?
videoQuiz3.explanation: Train leaves — in affirmative sentences we add -s to the verb for he / she / it.

videoQuiz3.text:
Now look at negative and question forms:
The train does not leave at 10.  Does the train leave at 10?
What auxiliary verbs do we use to make a negative sentence?
options: does not / doesn't  ·  do not / don't
am not / is not / are not
videoQuiz3.explanation: We use do not / don't with I, you, we, they — and does not / doesn't with he, she, it.

videoQuiz3.text: In questions and negatives with a 3rd person singular subject, does the verb still need an -s?
videoQuiz3.explanation: The -s moves to the auxiliary: does. The main verb stays in base form — The train does not leave (not “leaves”).

videoQuiz3b.text: Train _________ at 8 am in the morning.
videoQuiz3b.explanation: Train = it (3rd person singular) → add -s: arrives.
Guard’s doors = they (plural) → base form: open, no -s.
Train = it (3rd person singular) → add -s: leaves.
Train = it (3rd person singular) → does not + infinitive (no -s on the main verb).
Train = it (3rd person singular) → Does + subject + infinitive (no -s on the main verb).

videoQuiz4.text: Look at the sentences: “I am Yu”, “Yu is blind”, “I am not deaf”.
Do they describe an action or a quality?
videoQuiz4.explanation:
In Present Simple we use “to be” (am, is, are) verbs to describe qualities and characteristics like age, color, place, identity, or a state.
Examples: I’m 13. He is tall. They are at school.

videoQuiz4.text: Do these characteristics refer to the past, present or future?
Do the “to be” verbs change their form according to the subjects (I, He, We)?
videoQuiz4.explanation:
In Present Simple “to be” verbs change according to the subjects.
Iam a student
Youare a student
Heis a student
Sheis a student
Itis a book
Theyare pencils
Weare teachers

videoQuiz4.text: Reorder the words to make sentences:
Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order:
Do we use verbs after “am / is / are” in Present Simple?
videoQuiz4.explanation:
We do not use verbs after to be verbs in Present Simple. We use nouns, adjectives and other words to describe qualities and characteristics like age, color, place, identity, or a state.
Examples: I am fine. You are not tired. We are kids.

videoQuiz4.text: Choose the correct sentence:
Choose the correct sentence:

Now look at negative sentences:
I am not blind.   Yu is not deaf.
“Not” in the negative sentences stays…
options: Before the to be verb (not am, not is, not are)
After the to be verb (am not, is not, are not)
videoQuiz4.explanation:
“Not” stays after the to be verbs (am, is, are) in negative sentences.
Examples: You are NOT a doctor. She is NOT thirsty.

videoQuiz4.text: Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order:

Look at the dialogue:
– Are you deaf?
– No, I am blind.
What word starts the question? Fill in the blank:
videoQuiz4.explanation:
To make interrogative sentences with to be verbs we start the questions with “Am / Is / Are”.
Example: ARE you deaf?   What IS your name?

videoQuiz4.text: Where is the subject in the question?
Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz4.explanation: The word order for interrogatives with to be verbs is ASI = Auxiliary verb + Subject + quality words?

videoQuiz4.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz4.explanation: The word order for interrogatives with to be verbs is ASI = Auxiliary verb + Subject + quality words?

blocks.intro:
In English, the verbs to be are used to describe a person, thing, or state. Verbs to be change according to subjects.
am — “I”
is — 3rd person singular
are — two or more people and “you”

We make negative sentences by adding not to “am, is, are”.
Contractions:  is not = isn’t    are not = aren’t
Questions start with to be verbs “Am, Is, Are”.

taskDescription: Посмотрите на примеры из видео и ответьте на вопросы — они помогут лучше понять Present Simple в живой речи.

examples.dialogue: "My mama always said life was like a box of chocolates."
examples.translation: Моя мама всегда говорила, что жизнь - это как коробка конфет.

examples.dialogue: "I don't usually do this, but I like you."
examples.translation: Я обычно так не делаю, но ты мне нравишься.

examples.dialogue: "We don't have a lot of friends in common."
examples.translation: У нас нет много общих друзей.

taskExercises.question: В видео персонаж говорит о своей ежедневной рутине. Какое время правильно описывает привычные действия?
options: Present Continuous — I am eating breakfast
Present Simple — I eat breakfast every morning
Future Simple — I will eat breakfast
taskExercises.explanation: Present Simple используется для регулярных, привычных действий: "I eat breakfast every morning."

taskExercises.question: В видео прозвучала фраза "She works at a hospital." Почему глагол "works", а не "work"?
options: Потому что "she" — третье лицо ед. числа, добавляется -s
taskExercises.explanation: С подлежащими he / she / it в Present Simple глагол получает окончание -s или -es.

taskExercises.question: Какое слово из видео является маркером (сигнальным словом) Present Simple?
taskExercises.explanation: Слова always, usually, often, every day — типичные маркеры Present Simple, обозначающие регулярность.

taskExercises.question: В видео прозвучало отрицание. Выберите правильный вариант отрицания Present Simple для "he":
taskExercises.explanation: Для he/she/it отрицание строится: does not (doesn't) + базовая форма глагола без -s.

taskExercises.question: В видео задаётся вопрос о работе. Какой вспомогательный глагол нужен в вопросе с "she"?
taskExercises.explanation: В вопросе с he/she/it используется вспомогательный глагол "does": Does she work here?

exercises.question: Выберите правильное предложение:
options: He go to school every day.
He goes to school every day.
He going to school every day.
He is go to school every day.
exercises.explanation: С подлежащим "He" (третье лицо единственное число) глагол приобретает окончание -s: "goes"

exercises.question: Дополните предложение: "I __ breakfast at 7 AM."
exercises.explanation: Present Simple используется для описания регулярного действия. "I" не требует окончания -s.

exercises.question: Какое предложение верно?
exercises.explanation: С "she" (третье лицо) используется "does" для вопроса в Present Simple.

exercises.question: Завершите предложение: "She __ like coffee."
exercises.explanation: With "she" (third person singular), use "doesn't" or "does not" for negation.

exercises.question: Выберите правильный вариант: "They __ in the park on Sundays."
exercises.explanation: С "they" (множественное число) глагол не получает окончания -s: "play"

exercises.question: Какой вспомогательный глагол используется? "__ you understand English?"
exercises.explanation: С "you" используется "Do" для вопроса в Present Simple.

exercises.question: Дополните в отрицание: "He __ watch movies every night."
exercises.explanation: Для отрицания с третьим лицом: doesn't (does not) + базовая форма глагола.

exercises.question: Выберите грамотное предложение:
options: We doesn't go to school on Sundays.
We don't go to school on Sundays.
We not go to school on Sundays.
We aren't go to school on Sundays.

exercises.question: Какое утверждение правильно?
options: She write letters to her friend.
She writes letters to her friend.
She is writing letters to her friend.
She does writes letters to her friend.

exercises.question: Какой вариант содержит ошибку в грамматике?
options: The team play football twice a week.
She works as a doctor in the hospital.
exercises.explanation: "Team" может быть единственным или множественным. В американском английском "team plays" (единственное).

exercises.question: Составьте правильное отрицательное предложение:
options: They doesn't like swimming in winter.
They don't likes swimming in winter.
They don't like swimming in winter.
They not like swimming in winter.
exercises.explanation: С "they" используется "don't like", а не "doesn't like".

exercises.question: Выберите правильное соответствие подлежащего и глагола:
Какое предложение содержит правильный порядок слов в вопросе?
options: What time does start the class?
What time does the class start?
What time the class does start?
exercises.explanation: Порядок слов в вопросе с вспомогательным глаголом: Does + подлежащее + глагол.

exercises.question: Дополните: "Why __ she __ to work by bus?"
exercises.explanation: С "she" в вопросе: Does + she + базовая форма "go".
